pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Guice dependency to a version compatible with Java 17.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of Guice (e.g., 3.0) may rely on internal APIs or bytecode structures removed or changed in Java 17, causing runtime failures or compilation issues with newer toolchains.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update maven-compiler-plugin to a version that supports the 'release' flag and Java 17 compilation.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older versions of maven-compiler-plugin (e.g., 3.3) do not support the <release> configuration option introduced in Java 9, which is required for proper cross-compilation or targeting Java 17.

pom.xml.general_idea: Switch from separate source/target configuration to the unified release configuration for Java 17.
pom.xml.why_exists: The <source> and <target> tags are deprecated for Java 9+; using <release> ensures that the compiler uses the correct API and bytecode version for Java 17, preventing compilation errors or runtime linkage issues.
